response.justification: Maria Rodriguez has an extensive software career but is fundamentally misaligned with this role on multiple hard requirements. Most critically, there is no mention of Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a listed non-negotiable — which alone disqualifies the candidate. Beyond that, her background is overwhelmingly in web, mobile, and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, Ember.js, MongoDB), with negligible C programming exposure limited to a six-month stint at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project. There is no evidence of Linux kernel work, embedded firmware development, network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), or L2/L3 switching knowledge.
strengths: Long, continuous engineering career (15+ years) demonstrating sustained technical growth and adaptability across many domains
Some C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and in personal projects, showing baseline familiarity with lower-level languages
Demonstrated Linux usage and DevOps infrastructure experience (Docker, CI/CD, GitLab), providing a foundation that could be built upon
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency mentioned anywhere on the resume — a stated hard requirement for this role
No embedded systems, firmware, or network security experience; entire career is web/mobile/full-stack, making domain ramp-up cost prohibitive
C programming experience is surface-level and dated (2014–2015); role demands deep, production-grade C on UNIX with Linux kernel modification capability
